modelMetadata.name: Jack-Tar Deckhand — Presentation Engineering
modelMetadata.description: AI-First Business Service Architecture for a Claude Code skill suite that generates conference-quality PowerPoint presentations. Services are designed as independent, reusable domains orchestrated by a Deck Conductor agent.

services.name: Presentation Engineering
services.mission: Transform ideas into conference-quality visual presentations by orchestrating content, design, image, and assembly services.

services.name: Content Services
services.mission: Transform briefs and topics into structured narratives, outlines, and speaker-ready text.

services.name: Outline Generation
services.mission: Transform a talk brief into a structured slide outline with per-slide type, headline, body points, narrative beat, and visual direction.

services.name: Speaker Notes
services.mission: Generate timed, transition-cued speaker notes calibrated to talk duration and audience profile.

services.name: SmartArt Selection
services.mission: AI agent that recommends graphic type and enrichment tier for SmartArt-candidate slides. Negotiates with the narrative-architect through a multi-round proposal/approval loop: proposes 2-3 ranked recommendations per slide, narrative-architect evaluates against narrative arc and audience context, approves or rejects with feedback. Maximum 2 rejection rounds before fallback to composed strategy.

services.name: SmartArt Data Extraction
services.mission: Transform approved slide content into engine-specific structured data for SmartArt rendering. Deterministic extraction with LLM fallback for semantic parsing. Produces Mermaid syntax for graph-based types, Vega-Lite JSON for chart types, Matplotlib data for chart comparator, and custom SVG data structures for spatial types. Handles overflow via truncation, pagination, summarisation, or rejection policies. Validates output against target engine schemas.

services.name: Design Services
services.mission: Derive and enforce visual identity: palettes, typography, layout rules, and brand consistency.

services.name: Style Derivation
services.mission: Derive a complete StyleGuide from a TalkBrief and BrandProfile. When no BrandProfile is provided, derive minimal brand defaults. Proposes design options within brand compliance mode (strict or guided) and collaborates with the Speaker to select a direction. Produces palette, typography, layout templates, and image style tokens.

services.name: Brand Profile Management
services.mission: Create, store, and serve reusable BrandProfile artefacts from multiple input formats (brand guidelines PDF, corporate .pptx template, logo image, manual hex/font input, briefing document). BrandProfiles persist beyond a single deck session and are shared across multiple presentations for the same brand.

services.name: Layout Intelligence
services.mission: Apply machine-checkable layout rules: 12-column grid system, content zones, safe margins, visual hierarchy per slide type.

services.name: Image Services
services.mission: Generate, manipulate, and optimise visual assets from text prompts and data.

services.name: Image Routing & Discovery
services.mission: Entry point for all image generation. Discovers available providers at runtime and produces the AvailableProviders data contract — keyed per-model with supported_resolutions and pricing_backend metadata — consumed by image-cloud-generate, image-keynote-rendering, and deck-conductor for routing decisions. Routes to best model per asset type, budget, and per-model resolution capability, returns generated image.

services.name: Ollama Image Generation
services.mission: Generate raster images via local Ollama models (z-image-turbo, flux2-klein).

services.name: Ollama Icon Generation
services.mission: Generate icon and pictogram images via Ollama with icon-specific prompt wrapping.

services.name: Ollama Pattern Generation
services.mission: Generate seamless textures and repeating patterns via Ollama.

services.name: Ollama Diagram Generation
services.mission: Generate illustrative diagrams via Ollama. Honest about text quality limitations — use for conceptual visuals, not precise technical diagrams.

services.name: Cloud Image Generation
services.mission: Generate images via cloud APIs (OpenAI GPT Image 1.5, Google Imagen 4, Google Nano Banana Pro/Flash, FLUX.2 Pro) at requested resolution (1K / 2K / 4K) with provider routing based on availability, asset type, and per-model resolution support. Unsupported provider/model/resolution combinations raise ProviderResolutionUnsupportedError carrying the closest supported tier so callers can retry.

services.name: Cloud Icon Generation
services.mission: Generate SVG icons via Recraft V4 or raster icons via cloud models. SVG output is preferred for scalability.

services.name: Chart Rendering
services.mission: Generate publication-quality data visualisations via Matplotlib and Seaborn at 300 DPI, styled to the brand palette.

services.name: Image Post-Processing
services.mission: Background removal (rembg), resize, crop, colour correction, and file optimisation (pngquant) for generated images.

services.name: Slide Prompt Composition
services.mission: Assemble structured briefs from SlideOutline, StyleGuide, BrandProfile, and StrategyMap for each slide. Classifies slides into 5 rendering strategies (full_render, background, backdrop, pragmatic_composition, composed). For background strategy, selects one of 5 template zones (left_panel, right_panel, bottom_bar, top_band, center_float). For backdrop strategy, composes spatial-intent briefs with element layout metadata. For pragmatic_composition, splits the brief into 1 background prompt + N element prompts with shared style prefix. Briefs are consumed by the Prompt Engineer agent to produce image generation prompts. Handles brand constraint injection, resolution-aware formatting, and model-specific prompt hygiene. Validates that output prompts contain required elements.

services.name: Prompt Engineer
services.mission: AI Persona that receives structured briefs and produces creative image generation prompts. Composes spatial relationships, visual metaphors, and scene descriptions that template-based systems cannot. Supports 5 rendering strategies: atmospheric prompts (background), spatial-intent scene prompts with element arrangement (backdrop), element-level individual prompts with shared style prefix (pragmatic_composition), full-slide scene prompts (full_render), and no-image briefs (composed). Receives element metadata including element_layout regions and label_sources. Dispatched at Haiku by default for cost efficiency, escalated to Sonnet when output quality does not converge after 3 iterations.

services.name: Keynote Rendering
services.mission: Three-stage render funnel for keynote-quality slide images. Stage 1: Ollama draft (free, validate concept/composition). Stage 2: Cloud low-tier at 720p (cheap, validate on target model family, palette drift check). Stage 3: Cloud full-tier at 2K+ (production, single proven-prompt render). Supports 5 rendering strategies: full_render (complete slide as image), background (atmospheric image for template zone text overlay), backdrop (structured scene with vision post-analysis for element positioning), pragmatic_composition (1 background + N element images generated individually for deterministic assembly), and composed (no image generation, standard PptxGenJS). For backdrop strategy, triggers vision analysis after image generation. For pragmatic_composition, generates multiple images per slide with consistent style.

services.name: Image Generation Expert
services.mission: Advisory AI Persona for prompt engineering, model-specific translation, image quality scoring, iteration convergence guidance, and vision analysis quality advisory. For backdrop strategy slides, advises on whether vision-detected element positions are reliable (confidence thresholds) and recommends re-generation if element detection confidence is low.

services.name: Vision Analysis
services.mission: Post-generation vision analysis for backdrop strategy slides. Uses Claude vision to analyse generated images and detect where figurative elements (computers, nodes, process boxes) actually landed. Returns normalised bounding boxes {x, y, w, h, confidence} per detected element, ordered left-to-right then top-to-bottom. Confidence threshold 0.7 minimum; elements below threshold flagged for QA. Falls back to prescribed positions from element_layout when vision analysis fails or returns low confidence for all elements. Cost: ~$0.01-0.04 per analysis, applied only to backdrop slides (typically 1-3 per deck).

services.name: Image Reviewer
services.mission: Visual quality assessment for AI-generated presentation images. Dispatched per image after generation to check for artifacts, subject match, palette compliance, composition, and strategy fit. Returns a compact pass/refine verdict so the orchestration context never holds images. Runs at Haiku tier by default, escalates to Sonnet after 3 consecutive refine verdicts.

services.name: SmartArt Rendering
services.mission: Render SmartArtSpec data through three engines (Mermaid.js via CLI, Vega-Lite via CLI, custom SVG constraint engine) and composite with enrichment assets. In draft phase, runs comparator across eligible engines and dispatches image-reviewer for scoring. In production phase, renders via winning engine only. Handles four enrichment tiers: T0 pure programmatic SVG, T1 programmatic over AI background, T2 programmatic with AI element icons, T3 full AI render with data fidelity validation. Auto-downgrades T3 to T1 after 3 failed data fidelity checks.

services.name: Assembly & QA Services
services.mission: Compose content and visual assets into finished, validated presentation files.

services.name: PPTX Build
services.mission: Compose outline, style guide, image manifest, and speaker notes into a .pptx file via PptxGenJS. Routes to 5 assembly paths based on StrategyMap: buildFullRenderSlide() for full_render, buildBackgroundSlide() for background (5 template zone variants: left_panel, right_panel, bottom_bar, top_band, center_float), buildBackdropSlide() for backdrop (text at vision-detected coordinates with semi-transparent backing pills, reads detected_positions from ImageManifest), buildPragmaticSlide() for pragmatic_composition (background full-bleed + element images at exact prescribed coordinates + text labels adjacent to elements), and standard assembly for composed.

services.name: Visual QA
services.mission: Run 27 automated anti-pattern checks: contrast validation, margin enforcement, font size compliance, consistency audit, image quality thresholds. AP-27 (element layout validation): for backdrop and pragmatic_composition slides, validates all element regions within slide bounds (0.0-1.0), no two regions overlap by more than 10%, label_source references exist in SlideOutline, element count within 1-5 range. AP-28 (vision confidence): for backdrop slides, flags elements with vision detection confidence < 0.7 and slides where vision analysis fell back to prescribed positions. Existing AP-20 (overlap check) adjusted to not flag intentional text-over-image overlap for backdrop and pragmatic_composition strategies.

services.name: File Optimisation
services.mission: Image compression, font subsetting, metadata stripping, and file size management for the assembled .pptx.

services.name: Presentation Reviewer
services.mission: Review assembled decks against conference presentation best practices: narrative coherence, visual storytelling, pacing, speaker notes quality, audience appropriateness. For backdrop and pragmatic_composition slides, reviews text-to-element alignment quality — whether text labels are visually associated with the correct visual elements and whether the overall composition reads naturally.

services.name: Deck Conductor
services.mission: Orchestrate the full pipeline from talk brief to finished deck. Owns the DeckContext lifecycle, sequences L1 service invocations, manages budget, and handles the QA correction loop. Routes 5 rendering strategies (full_render, background, backdrop, pragmatic_composition, composed) through the pipeline, managing the additional vision analysis step for backdrop slides and multi-image generation for pragmatic_composition slides.

services.name: Superpower Bridge Services
services.mission: Wraps document-skills:pptx with narrative pre-brief and post-hoc visual enrichment. Composes existing image and SmartArt services without modifying /pptx.
services.description: Wraps document-skills:pptx with narrative pre-brief and post-hoc visual enrichment. Composes existing image and SmartArt services without modifying /pptx.

services.name: Narrative Brief Architect (AI)
services.mission: AI Persona — transforms topic + audience + duration into a structured creative brief that guides /pptx.
services.description: AI Persona — transforms topic + audience + duration into a structured creative brief that guides /pptx.

services.name: Enrichment Cohesion Reviewer (AI)
services.mission: AI Persona — reviews assembled enriched deck for cross-slide visual cohesion. Advisory verdicts only.
services.description: AI Persona — reviews assembled enriched deck for cross-slide visual cohesion. Advisory verdicts only.

services.name: Narrative Pre-Brief Skill
services.mission: Hosts /bridge-brief — Phase 1 of the bridge.

services.name: Deck Enrichment Skill
services.mission: Hosts /enrich-deck — Phase 3 of the bridge. Orchestration code, not a persona.
services.description: Hosts /enrich-deck — Phase 3 of the bridge. Orchestration code, not a persona.

aiPersonas.description: Top-level orchestration agent that sequences the presentation engineering pipeline. Receives a talk brief from the Speaker, invokes L1 services in dependency order (Design → Content → Image → Assembly → QA → Review), maintains shared state via the DeckContext file system, and manages cloud API budget. Routes 5 rendering strategies through the pipeline: full_render, background, backdrop, pragmatic_composition, composed. For backdrop slides, ensures vision analysis runs after image generation and before assembly. For pragmatic_composition slides, manages multi-image generation (1 background + N elements) and tracks per-element costs.

permitted: Parse and validate TalkBrief input
Generate deck plan (slide count, types, narrative flow)
Invoke any L1 service capability in any order
Create and manage DeckContext state directory (./tmp/deck/)
Invoke provider discovery and adapt routing based on available providers
Track cumulative cloud API spend against budget cap
Re-invoke services when QA or Reviewer identifies issues (max 2 correction cycles)
Report progress, cost, and timing to the Speaker
Route 5 rendering strategies (full_render, background, backdrop, pragmatic_composition, composed) through appropriate pipeline paths
Trigger vision analysis step for backdrop slides after image generation
Manage multi-image generation for pragmatic_composition slides (1 background + N element images)
Track vision analysis costs in budget alongside image generation costs
Invoke SmartArt selection negotiation between narrative-architect and smartart-selector
Invoke SmartArt data extraction after strategy-map approval
Invoke SmartArt rendering after enrichment images are generated
prohibited: Must not generate content, images, or style decisions directly — always delegates to L1 services
Must not spend above the declared budget cap without Speaker approval
Must not skip the QA step
Must not modify generated images or text — only re-invoke the producing service with corrective instructions
Must not proceed past provider discovery without confirming available capabilities to the Speaker
escalationTriggers: Budget will be exceeded by next generation request
QA finds critical issues after 2 correction cycles
Reviewer identifies structural narrative problems requiring Speaker input on direction
Talk brief is ambiguous or missing required fields
Vision analysis returns low confidence for all elements on a backdrop slide after re-generation
Pragmatic composition element style consistency is poor after re-generation with shared prompt prefix
SmartArt selector fails to produce acceptable recommendations after max negotiation rounds

dataSources.dataDescription: All DeckContext artefacts in ./tmp/deck/: StyleGuide, SlideOutline, ImageManifest, SpeakerNotes, ChartManifest, QAReport, PipelineState, StrategyMap, SmartArtRecommendations, SmartArtSpec, SmartArtManifest

aiPersonas.description: AI Persona that transforms structured slide briefs into creative image generation prompts. Composes spatial relationships, visual metaphors, typography descriptions, and scene layouts that pure template systems cannot produce. Supports 5 rendering strategies with strategy-specific composition rules: atmospheric prompts for background (mood/texture, no spatial requirements), spatial-intent scene prompts for backdrop (element arrangement descriptions like 'three computers arranged horizontally' without coordinate-level precision), element-level individual prompts for pragmatic_composition (1 background prompt + N element prompts with shared style prefix for visual consistency), full-slide scene prompts for full_render, and no prompts for composed. Receives element metadata including element_layout regions and label_sources in the structured brief. Dispatched at Haiku model by default for cost efficiency (~$0.001/call); escalated to Sonnet when output quality fails to converge after 3 iterations. Single agent definition, model selected at dispatch time.

permitted: Compose full-slide image generation prompts from structured briefs (full_render strategy)
Compose atmospheric background prompts with no spatial requirements (background strategy)
Compose spatial-intent scene prompts describing element arrangement without coordinate precision (backdrop strategy)
Compose element-level individual prompts: 1 background prompt + N element prompts with shared style prefix for visual consistency (pragmatic_composition strategy)
Adapt prompts for different target models (Ollama, OpenAI, Google, FAL)
Refine prompts based on vision review feedback scores
Include brand palette hex values and prohibited style constraints in prompts
Receive and incorporate element metadata (element_layout regions, label_sources) into spatial composition
prohibited: Must not generate images directly — only produces text prompts
Must not modify the StrategyMap or any DeckContext contract
Must not communicate with the Speaker — receives briefs from the composer, returns prompts
Must not make routing or budget decisions
escalationTriggers: Vision review scores below threshold after 3 prompt iterations at Haiku — escalate to Sonnet model
Prompt produces safety filter rejections from cloud API — escalate for alternative framing

dataSources.dataDescription: Per-slide brief containing headline, body text, visual direction, brand constraints, strategy, target resolution

aiPersonas.description: Advisory AI Persona consulted by image generation skills for prompt engineering, model-specific prompt translation, quality scoring against a 6-dimension rubric, iteration convergence guidance, and vision analysis quality advisory. For backdrop strategy slides, advises on whether vision-detected element positions are reliable based on confidence thresholds and recommends re-generation if element detection confidence is low. For pragmatic_composition slides, advises on style consistency across separately generated element images. Never generates images directly.

permitted: Advise on prompt construction for any image generation model (Ollama or cloud)
Translate visual_direction prose into model-specific prompts
Score generated images against 6-dimension rubric (composition, colour, clarity, relevance, technical quality, text accuracy)
Recommend iteration strategy (accept, refine prompt, rewrite, try different model)
Advise on model selection given asset type and available providers
Provide negative space and style consistency guidance
Advise on vision analysis quality for backdrop slides — assess whether detected element positions are reliable based on confidence scores
Recommend re-generation when vision detection confidence is below threshold (< 0.7) for critical elements
Advise on style consistency for pragmatic_composition element images — flag visual inconsistencies across separately generated elements
prohibited: Must not invoke image generation directly — only advises, the calling skill generates
Must not make routing decisions — the imagegen-bridge routes using the expert's advice
Must not access or modify DeckContext state
Must not communicate with the Speaker directly — all communication goes through the conductor or calling skill
escalationTriggers: Image quality score below threshold after max iterations — recommends fallback to next option in degradation chain
Prompt produces safety filter rejections from cloud API — recommends alternative prompt framing

dataSources.dataDescription: Palette for colour enforcement and brand consistency
Brand image style constraints: approved_image_styles and prohibited_image_styles for prompt compliance

aiPersonas.description: Visual quality reviewer for AI-generated presentation images. Dispatched per image after generation by the imagegen-bridge. Assesses against five criteria: artifacts (garbled text, mutations), subject match (vs visual_direction), palette compliance (vs brand palette), composition (open areas for text overlay), and strategy fit (standalone vs atmospheric). Returns a flat JSON verdict with pass/refine decision, confidence score, issues list, and one-line summary. Runs at Haiku tier for cost efficiency; escalates to Sonnet after 3 consecutive refine verdicts for more nuanced assessment. Never modifies images, refines prompts, or writes to DeckContext.

permitted: Read and visually assess generated image files
Check for visual artifacts: garbled text, extra limbs, impossible geometry, colour bleed, mutations
Assess whether image subject matches the visual_direction from the outline
Check dominant colour compliance against brand palette hex values
Assess composition suitability for the slide's rendering strategy
Return structured JSON verdict with pass/refine decision
Score SmartArt comparator candidates using adapted criteria (data legibility, layout clarity, aesthetic quality, style compliance)
Review SmartArt enrichment images for strategy fit (e.g., T1 backgrounds must have clear centre region for graphic overlay)
Assess data fidelity in T3 full-AI-render SmartArt images
prohibited: Must not generate or modify images
Must not refine or suggest prompts — that is the image-generation-expert's role
Must not write to DeckContext or manifest files
Must not communicate with Speaker directly
escalationTriggers: Image fails quality criteria after 3 consecutive assessments at Haiku tier — escalate to Sonnet for more nuanced review
Image accepted after 10 iterations with remaining issues — flag as accepted_with_issues in manifest

dataSources.dataDescription: visual_direction field for subject match assessment
Brand palette hex values for colour compliance checking

aiPersonas.description: Advisory AI Persona that reviews assembled decks against conference presentation best practices. Assesses narrative coherence, visual storytelling, pacing, speaker notes quality, and audience appropriateness. For backdrop and pragmatic_composition slides, reviews text-to-element alignment quality — whether text labels are visually associated with the correct visual elements and whether the overall composition reads naturally. Produces structured review with prioritised recommendations. Never modifies the deck directly.

permitted: Review assembled .pptx against conference presentation best practices
Assess narrative coherence for the stated audience and duration
Assess visual storytelling: slide transitions, image choices, visual rhythm
Assess pacing: slide density, breathing room, interaction beats
Assess speaker notes quality: specific and actionable vs generic filler
Produce structured review with per-slide feedback and overall assessment
Recommend specific improvements with priority (critical, suggested, polish)
Review text-to-element alignment quality for backdrop and pragmatic_composition slides
Assess whether visual element composition reads naturally and supports the slide's narrative intent
prohibited: Must not modify the deck, outline, images, or notes directly
Must not re-invoke any service — only the conductor acts on review feedback
Must not override the Speaker's creative choices — flags concerns but respects stated preferences
Must not duplicate deck-qa's programmatic checks — assumes those have already passed
escalationTriggers: Narrative structure fundamentally incompatible with stated format (e.g., 40 content slides for a 15-min lightning talk)
Visual identity inconsistent in ways programmatic QA cannot detect

dataSources.dataDescription: Finished .pptx file rendered to images for visual review
Original slide plan to check build matches intent
Brand and design parameters for consistency assessment
Speaker notes for pacing and quality review
Original talk brief for alignment checking

aiPersonas.description: AI agent that recommends graphic type and enrichment tier for SmartArt-candidate slides. Analyses slide content semantics, considers audience context, budget state, and adjacent slide strategies to propose 2-3 ranked recommendations per slide. Each recommendation includes graphic_type (from 10 v1 types: flowchart, decision_tree, bar_chart, line_chart, radar_chart, swot, feature_matrix, venn, timeline, pipeline_funnel, gantt, or none), enrichment_tier (pure_programmatic, ai_background, ai_elements, full_ai_render), target engine, rationale, and confidence score. Negotiates with narrative-architect through approval/rejection loop (max 2 rounds). Dispatched at Haiku for cost efficiency; escalated to Sonnet after 2 rejections.

permitted: Analyse slide content to determine appropriate graphic type
Recommend enrichment tier based on content type, audience, and budget
Consider adjacent slide strategies to avoid graphic fatigue
Propose 2-3 ranked graphic type + enrichment recommendations per slide
Accept feedback from narrative-architect rejection and adjust recommendations
Force enrichment to T0 (pure_programmatic) when budget state is degrade or typography_only
Recommend 'none' graphic type when slide content is not suited for SmartArt
prohibited: Must not extract structured data — that is the smartart-extractor's role
Must not render graphics — that is the smartart-renderer's role
Must not modify SlideOutline or any DeckContext contract
Must not communicate with Speaker directly
Must not make budget decisions — only considers budget state in recommendations
escalationTriggers: Narrative-architect rejects recommendations twice — escalate to Sonnet model
No suitable graphic type found for slide with explicit visual_intent — flag to conductor

dataSources.dataDescription: Full deck outline with visual_intent field for SmartArt candidate identification
Design tokens for feasibility assessment (can this graphic type work with this palette?)
Audience and tone context for enrichment tier selection

humanActors.name: Speaker
humanActors.role: Primary user. Provides the talk brief, makes creative decisions, reviews output, approves budget.

serviceAssociations.description: Initiates the pipeline by providing a TalkBrief
Reviews and approves extracted BrandProfile before it is used for style derivation
Receives the finished .pptx and review feedback
Selects narrative arc from options proposed by narrative-architect
Provides voice and style preferences to speaker-notes-writer

humanActors.name: Reviewer
humanActors.role: Optional human reviewer. Reviews finished deck for content accuracy, brand compliance, and overall quality before delivery.

serviceAssociations.description: Reviews QAReport and Presentation Review output

systemActors.name: Ollama
systemActors.description: Local AI model server for image generation. Provides z-image-turbo (fast photorealism) and flux2-klein (precision/text). Free, sequential generation only. Requires local installation and sufficient hardware.

serviceAssociations.description: Inference endpoint for raster image generation
Inference endpoint for icon generation
Inference endpoint for pattern generation
Inference endpoint for diagram generation

systemActors.name: OpenAI API
systemActors.description: GPT Image 1.5 — highest quality general-purpose image generation. Best text rendering, strong prompt adherence. $0.009-$0.133/image depending on quality tier. Resolution: 1K only (1024×1024 / 1536×1024 / 1024×1536); 2K/4K requests raise ProviderResolutionUnsupportedError. Requires OPENAI_API_KEY.

serviceAssociations.description: REST API for image generation

systemActors.name: Google Vertex AI
systemActors.description: Imagen 4 (Fast/Standard/Ultra) and Gemini 3 Image (Nano Banana Pro/Flash) — cost-effective image generation across the 1K/2K/4K ladder. Imagen 4 Fast at $0.02/image (1K only) is the budget workhorse for backgrounds; Nano Banana Flash supports 0.5K/1K/2K/4K ($0.045-$0.151); Nano Banana Pro supports 1K/2K/4K ($0.134-$0.24) for hero renders. Imagen pricing is dual-tier — flat per-image on Vertex (GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS) vs token-based on Gemini Developer API (GOOGLE_API_KEY); jack-tar-cloud auto-detects via env var. Requires GOOGLE_CLOUD_PROJECT + service account, OR GOOGLE_API_KEY for the Developer API path.

serviceAssociations.description: Vertex AI predict endpoint for image generation

systemActors.name: FAL.ai
systemActors.description: Unified access to 600+ models including FLUX.2 Pro (photorealism), Recraft V4 (SVG icons), and Ideogram 3.0 (typography). FLUX.2 Pro supports 1K/2K (caps at 2048²); 4K requests raise ProviderResolutionUnsupportedError. Single API key — recommended aggregation layer. Requires FAL_KEY.

serviceAssociations.description: Aggregated API for FLUX.2 and Ideogram models
Aggregated API for Recraft V4 SVG generation

systemActors.name: Recraft API
systemActors.description: Recraft V4 — the only AI model generating production-quality native SVG. Essential for scalable icons and brand assets. $0.04-$0.30/image. Requires RECRAFT_API_KEY. Also accessible via FAL.ai.

serviceAssociations.description: Direct API for SVG icon generation

systemActors.name: PptxGenJS
systemActors.description: Open-source PPTX generation library (v4.0.1, MIT). The rendering engine for assembling slides. Runs via Node.js in Claude Code. Supports slides, text, images, shapes, tables, charts, masters, speaker notes.

serviceAssociations.description: PPTX file generation engine

systemActors.name: Matplotlib
systemActors.description: Python charting library for headless data visualisation at 300 DPI. Uses Agg backend (no display server needed). Styled via .mplstyle files for brand consistency.

serviceAssociations.description: Chart rendering engine

systemActors.name: Mermaid CLI
systemActors.description: @mermaid-js/mermaid-cli (mmdc) for server-side Mermaid diagram rendering. Converts Mermaid DSL syntax to SVG without requiring a headless browser in the main process. Used for flowcharts, decision trees, Gantt charts, and other graph-based SmartArt types.

serviceAssociations.description: Mermaid syntax to SVG rendering engine

systemActors.name: Vega-Lite CLI
systemActors.description: vega-lite + vega-cli npm packages providing vl2svg for server-side Vega-Lite specification to SVG rendering. Used for bar charts, line charts, radar charts, and other data visualisation SmartArt types. Serves as comparator engine alongside Matplotlib for chart types.

serviceAssociations.description: Vega-Lite spec to SVG rendering engine

systemActors.name: Claude Vision API
systemActors.description: Claude vision capability for post-generation image analysis. Used by the vision analysis capability in Image Services to detect where figurative elements landed in generated backdrop images. Receives a generated image + element descriptions + expected element count. Returns normalised bounding boxes per detected element with confidence scores. Cost: ~$0.01-0.04 per analysis. Requires ANTHROPIC_API_KEY.

serviceAssociations.description: Vision analysis endpoint for element position detection in generated images

systemActors.name: Filesystem
systemActors.description: Project-local ./tmp/deck/ directory for DeckContext state persistence. JSON files for contracts, image files for generated assets, .pptx for output. Per CONSTITUTION.md Article 4.6, uses ./tmp/ not /tmp/.

serviceAssociations.description: State persistence for DeckContext
Output directory for .pptx files

interactions.description: Speaker provides TalkBrief to initiate the pipeline
Conductor requests Design Services: first brand-manager for BrandProfile, then slide-stylist for StyleGuide derivation
Conductor requests outline and speaker notes generation
Conductor requests image generation for each slide via imagegen-bridge
Conductor requests PPTX assembly, then QA, then Presentation Review
Conductor delivers finished .pptx, QAReport, review feedback, and cost report
imagegen-bridge probes Ollama availability at startup
imagegen-bridge checks OPENAI_API_KEY environment variable
imagegen-bridge checks GOOGLE_CLOUD_PROJECT environment variable
imagegen-bridge checks FAL_KEY environment variable
imagegen-bridge checks RECRAFT_API_KEY environment variable
Bridge routes to Ollama generation skills for local assets
Bridge routes to cloud generation skills for high-quality assets
Ollama skills call Ollama REST API for inference
Cloud skills call OpenAI API for GPT Image 1.5 generation. Resolution maps to native size kwarg (1K only — 2K/4K requests raise ProviderResolutionUnsupportedError before the call is made).

Cloud skills call Google for image generation. Imagen 4 (1K/2K) via Vertex AI predict endpoint or Gemini Developer API; Nano Banana Pro/Flash (1K/2K/4K) via Gemini generateContent endpoint with imageConfig.imageSize. Resolution kwarg is normalised to uppercase-K before dispatch.

Cloud skills call FAL.ai for FLUX.2 and Ideogram generation. Resolution maps to native image_size kwarg (preset for 1K, explicit width/height dict for 2K). Explicit caller-supplied image_size= overrides resolution-derived dict.
Cloud icon skill calls Recraft API for SVG generation
Cloud icon skill calls FAL.ai for Recraft V4 access
Chart renderer calls Matplotlib for headless chart generation
Assembler calls PptxGenJS to generate .pptx file
Generation skills consult the Image Generation Expert for prompt engineering and quality scoring
Cloud generation skills consult the expert for model-specific prompt translation
Bridge consults the expert for model selection advice given available providers
Conductor invokes the Presentation Reviewer after QA passes
QA returns findings to conductor for correction cycle (max 2 iterations)
Reviewer returns structured review to conductor for Speaker decision
Conductor invokes brand-manager before slide-stylist to obtain or create a BrandProfile
dataFlows: Brand assets (logo, PDF, .pptx template, hex/font input)

interactions.description: brand-manager provides BrandProfile to slide-stylist for StyleGuide derivation
Image Generation Expert reads BrandProfile for approved and prohibited image style constraints
Conductor presents design options to Speaker during Design phase for direction selection
Speaker selects narrative arc from 2-3 options proposed by narrative-architect
Conductor invokes slide prompt composer at Step 3.5 to produce the StrategyMap and structured briefs per slide
Conductor presents the StrategyMap to Speaker for approval. Speaker can override individual slide strategies or set approval_mode to one_shot.
Slide prompt composer dispatches structured briefs to the Prompt Engineer agent (Haiku default, Sonnet escalation) for creative prompt generation
Prompt Engineer returns generated image prompts to the composer for validation and storage in SlidePrompts contract
Keynote renderer invokes Ollama generation for Stage 1 draft renders (free, concept validation)
Keynote renderer invokes cloud generation for Stage 2 (low-tier 720p) and Stage 3 (full-tier 2K+) renders
Keynote renderer consults Image Generation Expert for vision-based quality scoring between funnel stages

Assembler reads StrategyMap to select assembly path per slide: full-bleed image (full_render), template zone overlay (background with backdrop_variant), vision-detected positioning (backdrop with detected_positions from ImageManifest), multi-image deterministic assembly (pragmatic_composition with element_layout), or programmatic (composed)

QA reads StrategyMap to select check suite per slide: vision-based checks for full_render, AP-27 element layout + AP-28 vision confidence for backdrop and pragmatic_composition, contrast-over-backdrop for background, standard checks for composed
Speaker provides voice and style preferences: note format, interaction style, experience level
For backdrop strategy slides, keynote renderer invokes vision analysis after image generation to detect where figurative elements landed in the generated scene image
Vision analysis capability calls Claude vision API with generated image and element detection prompt. Returns normalised bounding boxes per detected element with confidence scores.
Vision analysis writes detected_positions array to ImageManifest for the backdrop slide. Assembler reads detected positions to place text boxes at vision-detected coordinates.
dataFlows: detected_positions (element_id, x, y, w, h, confidence)

interactions.description: Vision analysis consults Image Generation Expert when element detection confidence is ambiguous — expert advises whether to accept positions, re-generate the image, or fall back to prescribed positions

For pragmatic_composition slides, slide prompt composer dispatches element-level briefs to the Prompt Engineer: 1 background brief + N element briefs with shared style prefix, element metadata, and label_sources

For pragmatic_composition slides, keynote renderer dispatches multiple generation requests through the image router: 1 background image + N element images. Each element image is generated individually via the same model for style consistency.
dataFlows: Model constraint (same model for all elements)

interactions.description: imagegen-bridge dispatches image-reviewer after each image generation for visual quality assessment
image-reviewer returns structured quality verdict to imagegen-bridge
Narrative-architect passes SlideOutline with visual_intent to SmartArt selector for graphic type recommendation
SmartArt selector returns 2-3 ranked graphic type + enrichment tier recommendations per slide
Narrative-architect approves or rejects SmartArt recommendations with feedback for retry
SmartArt extractor provides engine-specific structured data (Mermaid syntax, Vega-Lite JSON, custom SVG data) to the renderer
SmartArt renderer requests enrichment asset generation (AI backgrounds for T1, element icons for T2, full render for T3) via imagegen-bridge
imagegen-bridge provides generated enrichment images back to SmartArt renderer for compositing

SmartArt renderer dispatches image-reviewer for comparator scoring (draft phase) and enrichment quality review. Passes review_context (smartart_comparator, smartart_enrichment, or smartart_full_render) for criteria adaptation
SmartArt renderer provides SmartArtManifest to the assembler for buildSmartArtSlide() dispatch
SmartArt renderer invokes Mermaid CLI (mmdc) for graph-based diagram rendering
SmartArt renderer invokes Vega-Lite CLI (vl2svg) for data visualisation rendering

interactions.payload: image generation requests via image-bridge wrapper

dataContracts.name: StrategyMap
dataContracts.description: Per-slide rendering strategy classification. Persists in DeckContext as strategy-map.json. Determines which of 5 rendering strategies each slide uses: full_render (entire slide as AI image), background (atmospheric AI image + text in template zones), backdrop (structured AI scene + vision-detected text positioning), pragmatic_composition (AI-generated element images + deterministic assembly), or composed (standard PptxGenJS assembly). Retains backward compatibility with backdrop_render (maps to background with left_panel variant). Revised at any point during the pipeline — supports post-hoc single-slide upgrades.

fields.name: created_at
fields.description: ISO 8601 timestamp

fields.name: approval_mode
fields.description: Speaker approval mode: 'review' (default, approve before generation and before production) or 'one_shot' (pre-approved, skip approval gates)

fields.name: slides
fields.description: Per-slide strategy entries with slide_number, strategy (full_render|background|backdrop|pragmatic_composition|composed|backdrop_render), rationale, render_funnel, speaker_override

fields.name: slides[].backdrop_variant
fields.description: Template zone for background strategy: left_panel, right_panel, bottom_bar, top_band, center_float

fields.name: slides[].element_layout
fields.description: Element positioning for backdrop and pragmatic_composition strategies. Contains template name, elements[] with id/label_source/x/y/w/h, and title_region. For backdrop: target regions (vision may adjust). For pragmatic_composition: exact placement positions.

dataContracts.name: SlidePrompts
dataContracts.description: Generated image prompts per slide, produced by the Prompt Engineer agent from structured briefs. Stored as slide-prompts.json in DeckContext. Prompts are inspectable, editable, and reusable across decks. For pragmatic_composition slides, contains multiple prompts per slide: 1 background prompt + N element prompts with a shared_style_prefix for visual consistency.

fields.name: generated_at
fields.description: ISO 8601 timestamp

fields.name: prompts
fields.description: Per-slide prompt entries with slide_number, strategy, prompt_text, target_model, funnel_stage

fields.name: prompts[].element_prompts
fields.description: For pragmatic_composition: individual element prompts with element_id and prompt_text

fields.name: prompts[].shared_style_prefix
fields.description: For pragmatic_composition: shared style prefix applied to all element prompts for visual consistency

dataContracts.name: RenderLog
dataContracts.description: Append-only log of every render attempt across the pipeline. Captures prompt, model, resolution, vision score, iteration number, and cost. Tracks vision analysis costs for backdrop slides and per-element generation costs for pragmatic_composition slides. Builds best-practice dataset over time — which prompt patterns converge faster, which models handle text better, where Haiku vs Sonnet made the difference.

fields.name: entries
fields.description: Per-render entries with slide_number, strategy, funnel_stage, prompt_hash, model, resolution, vision_score, iteration, cost_usd, timestamp

fields.name: entries[].vision_analysis_cost_usd
fields.description: For backdrop slides: cost of the Claude vision analysis call (~$0.01-0.04)

fields.name: entries[].element_id
fields.description: For pragmatic_composition: identifies which element image this render entry is for (null for background image)

fields.name: entries[].vision_confidence
fields.description: For backdrop slides: average confidence score across all detected elements

dataContracts.name: BrandProfile
dataContracts.description: Reusable brand identity artefact that persists across deck sessions. Extracted from brand guidelines, templates, logos, or manual input. Consumed by slide-stylist for StyleGuide derivation and by Image Generation Expert for image style constraints.

fields.name: brand_id
fields.description: Unique identifier for the brand profile

fields.name: company_name
fields.description: Company or organisation name

fields.name: source_inputs
fields.description: List of input sources used to create this profile (e.g., 'logo.png', 'brand-guide.pdf', 'template.pptx')

fields.name: palette
fields.description: Brand colour palette with primary, secondary, accent, and neutral colours

fields.name: typography
fields.description: Brand fonts with heading, body, and monospace families plus weights

fields.name: approved_image_styles
fields.description: Image styles approved for brand use (e.g., 'flat illustration', 'corporate photography')

fields.name: prohibited_image_styles
fields.description: Image styles prohibited by brand guidelines (e.g., 'cartoon', 'stock photo clichés')

fields.name: compliance_mode
fields.description: Brand compliance enforcement level: 'strict' (exact colours/fonts only) or 'guided' (brand-inspired with creative latitude)

fields.name: extracted_at
fields.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of when the profile was created or last updated

dataContracts.name: AvailableProviders
dataContracts.description: Runtime manifest of which cloud and local image-generation providers are reachable in the current environment, what models each exposes, and what resolutions each model supports. Produced by provider_discovery.discover_providers() at the start of each pipeline run; consumed by the routing layer for model selection and by the cost estimator for tier selection.

fields.name: providers
fields.description: Keyed by provider id (openai | google | fal | recraft | ollama). Each value carries availability flag, missing-credential reason, and a models map.

fields.name: providers[].models
fields.description: Keyed by model id (e.g. gpt-image-1.5, gemini-3-pro-image-preview, gemini-3.1-flash-image-preview, imagen-4.0-fast-generate-001, fal-ai/flux-2-pro). Each value carries supported_resolutions, cost_table_ref, and capability flags.

fields.name: providers[].models[].supported_resolutions
fields.description: List of resolution tiers the model honours: subset of ["512", "1K", "2K", "4K"]. Routing must intersect this with the requested resolution before dispatch.

fields.name: providers[].models[].pricing_backend
fields.description: For Google Imagen models only: "vertex" (flat per-image) or "developer" (token-based), auto-detected from GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS vs GOOGLE_API_KEY env vars.

crossDomainSopRegister.rationale: Bridge consumes msft-smartart's engine.render + assembler_patch.inject named surface; msft-smartart owns the contract.

cac.ai_rm: Steve Jones (consolidated tripartite — to split as team grows)

crossDomainSopRegister.changeTrigger: Any breaking change to engine.render(spec, output_dir) signature/return shape OR assembler_patch.inject(host_pptx, requests) signature OR InjectionRequest field set requires CAC review before merge in jack-tar-msft-smartart. The bridge's DEP-BRIDGE-SMARTART-01 dependency-register entry pins the surface; surface change → version bump → CAC review.

dependencyRegister.surface: Presentation, ImagePart, slide.shapes.add_picture, slide.element XML access
